description: Compose a least-privilege subset chip (cartridge), mint a scoped principal, and spin a govd+exod body container for a subagent — registered to the fleet at a STRICTLY-lower fleet_tier than the deploying node. The anti-rogue path: a confined subagent cannot self-spawn a body, and this perk refuses any upward/sideways spawn or any chip/ACL that exceeds the parent's, fail-closed BEFORE any docker run.
rules: child fleet_tier must rank and be STRICTLY deeper than PARENT_FLEET_TIER (mothership>edge>subagent>…) — else reject fleet_tier_not_strictly_lower / fleet_tier_unknown
the child chip and the ACL skill set must each be a SUBSET of the parent chip — else reject acl_not_subset / chip_not_subset / acl_exceeds_chip
bearer/monitor tokens arrive ONLY as *_FILE pointers; only their sha256 is persisted (never a plaintext var, never the run-ledger)
the body runs with --entrypoint bypass + a wrapped {"principals":{...}} registry (the image entrypoint would otherwise mint agent-1 and clobber GOVD_PRINCIPALS)
on /health timeout the half-started container is stopped+removed and nothing is registered (no ghost row)
registration writes only the operator's ~/.cyberware roster, atomically — never the repo
usage: MODE=compose (subset PARENT_CHIP_DIR to the SKILLS list via cartridge) or MODE=mount (use a ready SKILL_DIR as-is, cartridge-verified). Required: NODE_NAME, CHILD_TIER, PARENT_FLEET_TIER, PARENT_CHIP_DIR, ACL_SKILLS, TOKEN_FILE (pointer). Optional: SKILLS|SKILL_DIR, PRINCIPAL_ID, MONITOR_TOKEN_FILE, PORT, IMAGE, ROLE, MAX_TIER, FLEET_FILE, CYBERWARE_ROOT. Output: ${RECORD_STORE}/deploy.json (the value-free deploy/refusal record).
limitation: Needs a running Docker daemon on a docker-capable cooperative host (a confined/exod body has no docker — the perk is inert there by design). Needs the cyberware repo importable for `python3 -m infra.tool.cartridge` (CYBERWARE_ROOT or the run cwd).
